item.title: 技术债务的 4 种类型：示例和修复 | The 4 Types of Tech Debt: With Examples and Fixes
item.description:
它是软件开发过程中不可避免的一部分，但我们必须妥善管理它。

在本文中，我将介绍……

  • 技术债务的四种类型
  • 代码债务的四种类型
  • 如何解决技术债务和代码债务
  • 技术债务示例

通过了解这些不同类型的技术债务以及如何管理它们，现代工程团队可以从根本上提高绩效。 这些性能提升包括代码质量、生产力、士气和减少技术债务的负面影响。

第一种技术债：代码债（大债）

代码债是软件开发团队在代码生产中偷工减料时积累的技术债。

当工程师使用快捷方式时，就会出现代码债务，从而增加必须在以后解决的复杂性。 随着时间的推移，这种复杂性可能很难得到回报，而且代价高昂，因为现有的技术债务和代码必须重构或用更好的解决方案替换。

在某些情况下，忽视解决代码债务可能会导致未来出现错误和安全问题。

四种类型的代码债务

著名软件开发专家 Martin Fowler 描述了四种类型的代码债务。 他发明了技术债务象限来解释技术债务的复杂问题以及如何对其进行分类。

这种类型的债务是由于缺乏对在代码中走捷径的后果的理解造成的。 然而，你故意走捷径而不考虑长期影响。

示例：您可以承担技术债务以满足紧迫的期限，但不要制定偿还计划。

  • 鲁莽和无意的债务：

这种针对技术债务的“鲁莽和无意的债务”方法指的是“作为开发人员应该知道但没有知道的事情”。 可以理解的是，单个开发人员并不了解他们领域中的一切。 但是，开发人员团队应该很好地了解他们的领域及其编码标准和最佳实践。 一个不断发展完善行业知识的团队可以避免积累这种债务。

示例：一组 JavaScript 开发人员没有应用他们在良好代码设计模式方面的知识。 该应用程序变得充满错误。

  • 谨慎和无意的债务：

这是您累积的技术债务，即使您花时间提高团队技能并实施最佳实践。 有时，技术债无法避免，只能通过做事来学习。 在其他情况下，随着 IT 行业的快速发展，您实施了过时的方法。

即使在实施衡量技术债务的指标时，也无法检测到此类债务。

为预测此类债务，您可以构建许多小型原型来测试新技术，然后再完全采用新方法或新技术。 通过这种方式，您可以获得有价值的知识而无需冒技术债务的风险，只有在新技术带来明显好处时才采用新技术。

  • 谨慎和故意的债务：

谨慎而深思熟虑的技术债务是您想要利用以加快交付速度的技术债务。 它源于深思熟虑的决策，平衡了潜在的风险和回报。 工程师和产品经理会考虑他们的选择可能带来的后果，并做出相应的计划，预测最坏的情况。

示例：初创公司可能会选择这种方法来快速将其 MVP 推向市场以验证产品并获得资金。 他们通过基本界面优先考虑其产品的核心功能。 他们计划在获得资金后加大对成品的投资。

如何修复代码库中的技术债务

您可以实施以下三种应对策略来避免代码库中的技术债务。

  • 跟踪高质量问题并确定优先级

良好的技术债务管理始于跟踪高质量问题。 如果您不知道自己有哪些技术债务，就无法确定优先级并修复它。 通过修复影响最大的问题，您可以提高代码库的质量并防止引入新问题。

使用正确的工具来跟踪高优先级问题至关重要。 它使我更容易识别和跟踪高质量问题，而无需切换工具并为新问题添加大量上下文。 通过直接在我的 IDE 中创建问题，上下文会自动添加并变得对我团队中的其他开发人员更加可见。

将每个冲刺的 15-20% 分配给技术债务

持续分配资源来解决现有的技术债务。 它使您能够快速响应技术债务的积累，减少技术债务并确保代码保持可维护性和高效性。 您可以使用这些资源来修复错误、升级代码架构或提高代码性能。

关注直接影响代码质量、士气、性能或安全性的问题。 我建议为每个冲刺选择一个主题。

像 Stepsize 这样的工具可以帮助您可视化代码库中的技术债务并根据主题过滤问题。

修复更容易、唾手可得的成果可能很诱人。 但是，这些问题对代码质量的影响可以忽略不计。 它们甚至可能因不优先考虑高质量问题而产生不利影响。

为了随着代码库的增长保持功能速度，您应该在每个冲刺中投入 15-20% 的资源来解决技术债务。

  • 自动监控技术债务

技术债务象限显示“谨慎和无意的债务”仍然可能发生。 但是，我们可以通过实施度量标准来检测其他三种类型的代码债务。

首先，您可以衡量一些有价值的指标：代码复杂性、代码重复、代码改动和错误密度。 最后一个指标特别有价值，因为它表明每单位代码的错误数量是否增加。 如果您注意到代码库的特定区域有所增加，请仔细分析代码质量以了解为什么会出现更多错误。 自动化测试可以帮助您发现这些问题。

第二种技术债务：文档

文档是您产品的一个重要方面，它提供有关其设计、核心逻辑和功能的信息。

不准确或不完整的文档可能会导致团队成员之间的混淆或误解。

例如，当您的文档过时时，开发人员可能会对代码做出错误的假设。 然后他们不小心引入了错误或新的代码债务。

通过实施文档流程避免文档债务。 定期审查您的文档或每次发布可确保您的文档与您的代码保持同步。

尝试使用像Swimm这样的工具来简化从编辑器创建文档的过程。

第三种技术债务：安全

当您在软件开发过程中积累安全漏洞时，您就会积累安全技术债务。 如果恶意行为者利用它，忽视这种类型的技术债务可能会给您的公司及其客户带来严重后果。

解决安全技术债务的最佳实践涉及主动风险管理、定期安全审查、持续测试和监控以及持续教育。

第四种技术债务：工程工具

工程工具是指您在工程团队中使用的各种工具和技术。 不记录与您的工程工具相关的基本过程可能会导致工程工具债务。

例如，您需要一份描述如何标记和发布新代码版本的正式流程文档。 否则，每个开发人员都有自己的代码发布方法，从而造成犯错的机会之窗。 举一个具体的例子，工程师可能会忘记执行端到端测试，从而在不知不觉中发布包含错误的代码。

写下与工程工具相关的重要过程并教工程师如何使用该工具至关重要。

包起来

一旦遇到技术债务，它可能会持续存在。 您应该注意多种类型的技术债务。 最明显的技术债务类型是代码债务。 但是，您也可能遇到文档、安全或工程工具方面的债务。

现代工程团队绝对应该积累技术债务。 应该有意识和谨慎地承担技术债务。 使用正确的工具正确管理技术债务。

它使他们能够跟踪、管理和修复技术债务，以利用竞争优势。

Learn how you can use Stepsize to transform your engineering team's approach to tech debt here. 在此处了解如何使用 Stepsize 转变工程团队处理技术债务的方法。



原文:

“Technical debt” describes the trade-off between delivering new features quickly and having a robust, reliable system.

It’s an inevitable part of the software development process, but we must manage it properly.

In this article, I’m going to cover…

  • The four types of technical debt
  • The four types of code debt
  • How to fix tech debt and code debt
  • Tech debt examples

By understanding these different types of tech debt and how to manage them, modern engineering teams can radically improve performance. Those performance gains include code quality, productivity, morale and reducing the negative impacts of technical debt.

The First Type of Tech Debt: Code Debt (the big one)

Code debt is the technical debt that accumulates when software development teams cut corners in code production.

Code debt occurs when engineers use shortcuts, increasing complexity that must be addressed later. This complexity can be difficult and expensive to pay off over time, as existing technical debt and code must be refactored or replaced with a better solution.

In some cases, neglecting to address code debt can lead to bugs and security issues in the future.

Four Types of Code Debt

Martin Fowler, a well-known software development expert, has described four types of code debt. He invented the technical debt quadrant to explain the complex matter of technical debt and how to classify it.

This type of debt results from a lack of understanding of the consequences of taking shortcuts in the code. However, you deliberately take shortcuts without considering the long-term implications.

Example: You can take on technical debt to meet tight deadlines, but don’t create a plan to pay it back.

  • Reckless & Inadvertent Debt:

This “Reckless & Inadvertent Debt” approach to technical debt refers to “things you should have known as a developer but didn’t”. Understandably, an individual developer doesn’t know everything in their domain. However, a team of developers should understand their domain well and its coding standards and best practices. A team constantly developing sound industry knowledge can avoid accumulating this kind of debt.

Example: A team of JavaScript developers don’t apply their knowledge of good code design patterns. The application becomes riddled with bugs.

  • Prudent & Inadvertent Debt:

This is tech debt you have accrued even though you took time to improve your team’s skills and implement best practices. Sometimes, technical debt can’t be avoided and can only be learned by doing things. In other situations, you’ve implemented outdated approaches as the IT industry moves forward rapidly.

Martin Fowler states this is the hardest quadrant of technical debt to anticipate. It’s impossible to detect this type of debt even when implementing metrics to measure technical debt.

**Example: **You migrate your application architecture to a monolith while the industry moves towards a microservices architecture. To anticipate this type of debt, you can build many small prototypes to test new technologies before fully committing to a new approach or technology. This way, you can gain valuable knowledge without risking technical debt, only adopting a new technology when it offers clear benefits.

  • Prudent & Deliberate Debt:

Prudent and deliberate tech debt is the technical debt you want to leverage to ship faster. It results from thoughtful decision-making, balancing the potential risks and rewards. Engineers and product managers consider the possible consequences of their choices and plan accordingly, anticipating worst-case scenarios.

Example: A startup might opt for this approach to quickly bring their MVP to market to validate the product and secure funding. They prioritise the core functionality of their product with a basic interface. They plan to invest more in a polished product when they secure funding.

How to Fix Technical Debt in Your Codebase

Here are three coping strategies you can implement to avoid technical debt in your codebase.

  • Track and prioritise high-quality issues

Good tech debt management starts with tracking high-quality issues. If you don’t know what technical debt you have, you can’t prioritise and fix it. By fixing the most impactful issues, you can improve the quality of your codebase and prevent introducing new problems.

It’s essential to use the right tools to track high-priority issues. Stepsize helps me create and manage issues directly from my code editor. It makes it easier for me to identify and track high-quality issues without switching tools and adding a lot of context to a new issue. By creating issues directly in my IDE, context gets automatically added and becomes more visible to other developers in my team.

Try Stepsize here for free – it’s the ideal solution for modern engineering teams.

  • Allocate 15-20% of every sprint to tech debt

Continuously allocate resources to addressing existing technical debt. It allows you to respond quickly to technical debt buildup, reduce technical debt and ensure the code remains maintainable and efficient. You can use these resources to fix bugs, upgrade the code architecture, or improve code performance.

Focus on issues that directly impact code quality, morale, performance, or security. I suggest choosing a theme for each sprint.

A tool like Stepsize can help you visualise tech debt in your codebase and filter issues based on a theme.

It might be tempting to fix easier, low-hanging fruit. However, these issues have a negligible impact on code quality. They might even be undone by the adverse effects of not prioritising high-quality issues.

Technical debt can slow down development processes, making it difficult to deliver new features. To maintain feature velocity as your codebase grows, you should commit 15-20% of your resources to address technical debt each sprint.

  • Automate the monitoring of technical debt

The technical debt quadrant showed that “Prudent & Inadvertent Debt” can still occur. However, we can detect the three other types of code debt by implementing metrics to measure them.

To get started, you can measure a few valuable metrics: code complexity, code duplication, code churn, and bug density. This last metric is especially valuable because it tells if the number of bugs per unit of code increases. If you notice an increase in a specific area of your codebase, analyse code quality carefully to understand why more bugs occur. Automated tests can help you spot these issues.

The Second Type of Tech Debt: Documentation

Documentation is an essential aspect of your product and provides information about its design, core logic, and functioning.

Inaccurate or incomplete documentation can lead to confusion or misunderstandings among your team members.

For instance, developers might make wrong assumptions about the code when your documentation is outdated. They then accidentally introduce bugs or new code debt.

Avoid documentation debt by implementing a documentation process. Regularly reviewing your documentation or for each release ensures your documentation stays in sync with your code.

Try a tool like Swimm to make creating documentation from your editor easy.

The Third Type of Tech Debt: Security

You accumulate security tech debt when you accrue security vulnerabilities through your software development process. Neglecting this type of technical debt can have severe consequences for your company and its customers if malicious actors exploit it.

The best practices for addressing security technical debt involve proactive risk management, regular security reviews, continuous testing and monitoring, and continuous education.

The Fourth Type of Tech Debt: Engineering Tooling

Engineering tooling refers to the various tools and technologies you use within your engineering team. Not documenting essential processes related to your engineering tooling may lead to engineering tooling debt.

For instance, you need a formal process document describing how to tag and release a new code version. Otherwise, each developer has their own approach to releasing code, creating a window of opportunity for mistakes. To give a concrete example, an engineer might forget to execute end-to-end tests, unknowingly releasing code that contains bugs.

It’s crucial to write down important processes related to engineering tooling and teach engineers how to use the tool.

Wrapping Up

Technical debt can be persistent once you encounter it. There are many types of technical debt you should look out for. The most apparent type of technical debt is code debt. However, you can also encounter documentation, security, or engineering tool debt.

Modern engineering teams absolutely should accumulate tech debt. That tech debt should be taken on both deliberately and prudently. Manage tech debt properly using the right tools.

Stepsize developed a specialised tool for high-performing engineering teams. It enables them to track, manage and fix technical debt to leverage a competitive advantage.

Learn how you can use Stepsize to transform your engineering team’s approach to tech debt here.
item.author: alexomeyer

item.title: 表单输入验证：确保问卷和在线表单中的响应有效、有意义且可靠 | Form Input Validation: Ensure Valid, Meaningful, and Reliable Responses in Questionnaires and Online Forms
item.description:
了解如何设计有效的问卷和配置表单字段验证以提高调查结果的质量。

精心设计的问卷可以提高其完成率并提供积极的用户体验。 如果您的调查清晰、结构合理且问题简明扼要，调查人员将愿意提供相关且可操作的数据，您可以使用这些数据获得有价值的见解。

但是你如何做到这一点？ 您如何组织一份在线问卷，既不会让受访者不知所措，又能让他们的体验愉快？

适当的完成时间

设计问卷需要仔细考虑各种因素。 其中之一是完成时间，尤其是在涉及在线调查时。 为了最大限度地提高参与率，请将调查限制在 10 分钟以内，对于移动设备用户甚至更短 - 大约 7 分钟。

合适的题型

另一个关键方面是选择适当的问题类型，以确保收集的数据适合进一步分析和可视化表示。 请参阅如何创建有效的调查、投票或测验并提高其完成率一文以了解有关此主题的更多信息。

相关回应

数据有效性是设计问卷时要考虑的另一个关键因素。 为确保数据可靠，请确定您想要和不想接收的数据类型。 这种方法可以通过澄清预期从受访者那里获得的数据来帮助避免不确定性。

不同的字段需要不同类型的验证：必填字段的验证、年龄或体重值的数字范围验证以及特定文件格式（如 PDF、JPEG 或 MP4）上传的文件类型验证。

使用SurveyJS ，您可以将这些和其他数据验证设置应用于您的调查问题，确保受访者提供准确可靠的数据。 在以下部分中，我们将回顾 SurveyJS 可用的一些可能的数据验证选项，这有助于提高调查数据的质量。

使用 SurveyJS 进行表单输入验证

强制性问题

将isRequired属性值设置为true以确保用户在提交调查之前不会留下未回答的问题。

 { "type": "text", "name": "last-name", "title": "Last name", "isRequired": true }


演示：COVID-19 筛查表

如果您使用 单选矩阵并希望确保用户解决了行中的所有问题，请启用isAllRowRequired选项。


限制选择的数量

如果您想限制受访者在多选复选框和标签框问题中可以选择的选项数量，您可以使用maxSelectedChoices属性。

 { "type": "checkbox", "name": "product-aspects", "title": "Key Features", "description": "These are some important aspects of the product. Select three features you find most important.", "choices": [ "Technical support", "Price", "Delivery option", "Quality", "Ease of use", "Product warranties" ], "maxSelectedChoices": 3 }

一旦用户选择了允许的最大项目数，则该问题的进一步选择将被禁用。

数值范围

您可以使用min和max属性为需要数字输入类型的问题设置数字范围。

 { "name": "number", "type": "text", "title": "Enter a number from 0 to 100", "inputType": "number", "min": 0, "max": 100, "defaultValue": 0, "isRequired": true }

演示：数字输入

文本长度

通过设置文本输入字段的最小或最大长度值，您可以控制用户可以输入的字符数。

最小文本长度值有助于确保用户提供足够的数据并防止他们提交无效且最终无用的响应。

 { "type": "comment", "name": "service_improvements", "title": "How can we improve our service?", "validators": [ { "type": "text", "text": "Please provide more information", "minLength": 10 } ] }

另一方面，设置最大文本长度值可以帮助防止用户在他们的答案中提交多余的细节。 这将使调查答复简明扼要并侧重于相关信息。

 { "type": "comment", "name": "service_improvements", "title": "How can we improve our service?", "maxLength": 100 }

最大文件大小

您可以限制可作为问题答复上传的文件的大小。

 { "type": "file", "title": "Please upload your files", "name": "files", "storeDataAsText": false, "allowMultiple": true, "maxSize": 102400 }

演示：文件问题 - 将文件上传到服务器

接受的文件类型

配置文件问题类型时，使用acceptedTypes属性来限制可以上传的文件类型。

常用表达

您可以使用正则表达式来实现更复杂的逻辑并验证电子邮件地址、电话号码或密码等输入值。

 { "name": "user-password", "type": "text", "title": "Password", "inputType": "password", "description": "Enter a password that contains at least one upper-case, one lower-case letter, a digit, and a special character", "isRequired": true, "validators": [ { "type": "regex", "regex": /(?=.*\d)(?=.*[az])(?=.*[AZ])(?!.*\s)(?=.*[!-@#$*])/, "text": "Your password must contain at least one upper-case, one lower-case letter, a digit, and a special character" } ] }

演示：表单验证

自定义验证函数

验证函数可用于验证更详细的响应。 例如，当涉及到电子邮件地址时，您可能不仅要验证输入的值是否符合电子邮件格式，还要验证它是否属于特定公司的域。

演示：使用事件的自定义表单验证

服务器端验证

除了客户端验证之外，还使用服务器端验证来防止恶意提交或错误。

概括

请考虑以下建议，以确保受访者在参加您的调查时提供准确的数据。

验证所有字段

确保所有字段都已验证，包括可选字段。 这有助于防止提交不完整和错误，并确保提供的大部分信息有效。

提供帮助文本

此外，您可以自定义错误消息并提供有关修复错误的明确说明。 使用通俗易懂的语言，避免使用技术术语。

使用实时验证

实时验证可帮助用户在键入时纠正错误，而不是等到提交表单。 将checkErrorsMode属性设置为onValueChanged以在用户提供答案后立即验证用户响应。

最后，始终与一小群人（理想情况下 - 来自预期的目标群体）一起测试您的调查。 这可确保调查清晰易懂，并有助于在调查到达主要目标受众之前发现并纠正问题和选项中的错误或含糊之处。

结论

设计有效的调查需要技巧和创造力。 SurveyJS offers a wide selection of tools to help you create more engaging and insightful surveys and online forms. SurveyJS 提供了多种工具来帮助您创建更具吸引力和洞察力的调查和在线表格。



原文:

Learn how to design an effective questionnaire and configure form field validation to improve the quality of your survey results.

A thoughtfully designed questionnaire can improve its completion rate and deliver a positive user experience. If your survey is clear and well structured, and questions are concise, survey takers will be willing to provide relevant and actionable data that you can use to derive valuable insights.

But how do you accomplish this? How do you organize an online questionnaire that doesn't overwhelm respondents and instead makes their experience enjoyable?

Proper Completion Time

Designing a questionnaire requires careful consideration of various factors. One of them is completion time, especially when it comes to online surveys. To maximize participation rates, limit the survey to no more than 10 minutes, or even shorter for mobile device users - around 7 minutes.

Suitable Question Types

Another critical aspect is selecting appropriate question types to ensure that the data collected will be suitable for further analysis and visual representation. Please refer to the How to Create an Effective Survey, Poll, or Quiz, and Increase its Completion Rate article to find out more on this topic.

Relevant Responses

Data validity is another key factor to consider when designing a questionnaire. To ensure reliable data, identify the types of data you want and do not want to receive. This approach can help avoid uncertainties by clarifying the data expected from respondents.

Different fields require different types of validation: validation for mandatory fields, numeric range validation for age or weight values, and file type validation for uploads in a specific file format, such as PDF, JPEG, or MP4.

Using SurveyJS, you can apply these and other data validation settings to your survey questions, ensuring that respondents provide accurate and reliable data. In the following sections, we will review some possible data validation options available with SurveyJS, which can help improve the quality of your survey data.

Form Input Validation with SurveyJS

SurveyJS is an open-source JavaScript library for building complex forms and surveys in React, Angular, Vue.js, Knockout, and jQuery. SurveyJS offers a variety of input validation options that can help ensure that respondents provide valid answers.

Mandatory Questions

Set the isRequired property value to true to ensure that a user doesn't leave a question unanswered before submitting a survey.

{
  "type": "text",
  "name": "last-name",
  "title": "Last name",
  "isRequired": true
}


Demo: COVID-19 Screening Form

If you use Single-Choice Matrix and you want to ensure that a user addresses all question in the rows, enable the isAllRowRequired option.


Demo: Single-Selection Matrix

Restrict the Number of Choices to Select

If you want to limit the number of choice options a respondent can select in Multiple-Selection Checkbox and TagBox questions, you can use the maxSelectedChoices property.

{
  "type": "checkbox",
  "name": "product-aspects",
  "title": "Key Features",
  "description": "These are some important aspects of the product. Select three features you find most important.",
  "choices": [
    "Technical support",
    "Price",
    "Delivery option",
    "Quality",
    "Ease of use",
    "Product warranties"
  ],
  "maxSelectedChoices": 3
}

Once a user selects the maximum number of items allowed, further selection is disabled for that question.

Numeric Ranges

You can use the min and max properties to set numeric ranges for questions that require a numerical input type.

{
  "name": "number",
  "type": "text",
  "title": "Enter a number from 0 to 100",
  "inputType": "number",
  "min": 0,
  "max": 100,
  "defaultValue": 0,
  "isRequired": true
}

Demo: Numeric Entry

Text Length

By setting the minimum or maximum length values for text entry fields, you can control the number of characters users can enter.

The minimum text length value can help ensure that users provide enough data and prevent them from submitting invalid and eventually useless responses.

{
  "type": "comment",
  "name": "service_improvements",
  "title": "How can we improve our service?",
  "validators": [
    {
      "type": "text",
      "text": "Please provide more information",
      "minLength": 10
    }
  ]
}

Setting the maximum text length value, on the other hand, can help prevent users from submitting redundant details in their answers. This will keep survey responses concise and focused on relevant information.

{
  "type": "comment",
  "name": "service_improvements",
  "title": "How can we improve our service?",
  "maxLength": 100
}

Maximum File Size

You can limit the size of files that can be uploaded as responses to questions.

{
  "type": "file",
  "title": "Please upload your files",
  "name": "files",
  "storeDataAsText": false,
  "allowMultiple": true,
  "maxSize": 102400
}

Demo: File question - Upload Files to a Server

Accepted File Types

When configuring a File question type, use the acceptedTypes property to restrict the file types that can be uploaded.

Regular Expressions

You can use regular expressions to implement more sophisticated logic and validate such input values as email addresses, phone numbers, or passwords.

{
  "name": "user-password",
  "type": "text",
  "title": "Password",
  "inputType": "password",
  "description": "Enter a password that contains at least one upper-case, one lower-case letter, a digit, and a special character",
  "isRequired": true,
  "validators": [
    {
      "type": "regex",
      "regex": /(?=.*\d)(?=.*[a-z])(?=.*[A-Z])(?!.*\s)(?=.*[!-@#$*])/,
      "text": "Your password must contain at least one upper-case, one lower-case letter, a digit, and a special character"
    }
  ]
}

Demo: Form Validation

Custom Validation Functions

Validation functions can be useful in verifying more detailed responses. For instance, when it comes to email address, you may want to validate not only that the entered value conforms to the email format but also that it belongs to the domain of a specific company.

Demo: Custom Form Validation Using an Event

Server-Side Validation

Use server-side validation in addition to client-side validation to prevent malicious submissions or errors.

Demo: Server-Side Form Validation Using an Event

Summary

Consider the following recommendations to ensure that respondents provide accurate data while taking your survey.

Validate All Fields

Ensure that all fields have validation on, including optional fields. This helps prevent incomplete submissions and errors and ensures that most of the information provided is valid.

Provide Help Text

SurveyJS highlights a field with an error and displays an error message next to this field.

In addition, you can customize error messages and provide clear instructions on fixing the error. Use plain language and avoid technical jargon.

Use Real-Time Validation

Real-time validation helps users correct errors as they type rather than waiting until the form is submitted. Set the checkErrorsMode property to onValueChanged to validate user responses as soon as users provide answers.

Lastly, always test your survey with a small group of people (ideally - from the intended target group). This ensures that the survey is clear and easy to understand and can help detect and correct errors or ambiguities in questions and choice options before it reaches the main target audience.

Conclusion

Designing effective surveys requires both skill and creativity. SurveyJS offers a wide selection of tools to help you create more engaging and insightful surveys and online forms.
item.author: surveyjs

item.title: 量子计算如何彻底改变人工智能算法 | How quantum computing can revolutionise AI algorithms
item.description:
量子计算有可能以多种方式彻底改变人工智能算法。 通过利用量子力学原理，量子计算机可以以目前经典计算机无法达到的速度处理和分析大量数据。 以下是量子计算可以显着改进 AI 算法的一些方式：

更快的处理和训练：量子计算机使用量子位而不是经典位，这允许它们同时执行多个计算。 这种并行性使量子计算机能够比传统计算机更快地解决复杂问题和处理大型数据集。 因此，可以更快、更高效地训练人工智能模型。

增强优化：许多 AI 问题，例如组合优化，都可以映射到量子计算框架上。 量子计算机可以同时探索多个解决方案，比经典计算机更有效地找到最优解。 此功能可以增强依赖优化的 AI 算法，例如机器学习和运筹学中使用的算法。

提高准确性和精度：量子计算可以通过减少数据处理中的错误和噪声来提高人工智能算法的准确性和精度。 量子纠错技术可以帮助最大限度地减少计算过程中可能出现的噪声和其他错误的影响，从而产生更可靠的 AI 模型。

量子机器学习：量子计算可以开发新的量子机器学习算法，利用量子现象，如纠缠和叠加。 这些算法可以增强机器学习模型的性能，使它们能够学习复杂的模式并更有效地进行预测。

解决棘手问题：由于解决这些问题所需的计算资源呈指数级增长，一些问题被认为对于经典计算机来说是棘手的。 量子计算有可能解决这些问题，为人工智能算法在密码学、优化和模拟等领域开辟新的可能性。

扩展 AI 能力：量子计算的集成可以让 AI 算法解决以前认为无法解决的问题。 例如，量子计算可能使 AI 模型能够模拟量子系统，从而在材料科学、药物发现和气候建模等领域开辟新的研究机会。

总的来说，将量子计算集成到人工智能算法中可以产生更快、更高效、更准确的模型。 随着量子计算技术的不断进步，我们可以期待在各个领域看到 AI 能力和应用的显着改进。



原文:

Quantum computing has the potential to revolutionise AI algorithms in several ways. By exploiting the principles of quantum mechanics, quantum computers can process and analyse vast amounts of data at speeds that are currently unattainable by classical computers. Here are some ways quantum computing can bring about significant improvements in AI algorithms:

Faster processing and training: Quantum computers use qubits instead of classical bits, which allows them to perform multiple calculations simultaneously. This parallelism enables quantum computers to solve complex problems and process large datasets much faster than classical computers. As a result, AI models can be trained more rapidly and efficiently.

Enhanced optimization: Many AI problems, such as combinatorial optimization, can be mapped onto quantum computing frameworks. Quantum computers can explore multiple solutions concurrently, allowing them to find the optimal solution more effectively than classical computers. This capability can enhance AI algorithms that rely on optimization, such as those used in machine learning and operations research.

Improved accuracy and precision: Quantum computing can potentially improve the accuracy and precision of AI algorithms by reducing errors and noise in data processing. Quantum error correction techniques can help minimize the impact of noise and other errors that may arise during computation, leading to more reliable AI models.

Quantum machine learning: Quantum computing can enable the development of new quantum machine learning algorithms that exploit quantum phenomena, such as entanglement and superposition. These algorithms can enhance the performance of machine learning models, enabling them to learn complex patterns and make predictions more effectively.

Solving intractable problems: Some problems are considered intractable for classical computers due to the exponential growth of computational resources required to solve them. Quantum computing has the potential to tackle these problems, opening up new possibilities for AI algorithms in areas like cryptography, optimization, and simulation.

Expanding AI capabilities: The integration of quantum computing can allow AI algorithms to solve problems previously considered out of reach. For example, quantum computing can potentially enable AI models to simulate quantum systems, opening up new research opportunities in fields like materials science, drug discovery, and climate modeling.

Overall, the integration of quantum computing into AI algorithms can lead to faster, more efficient, and more accurate models. As quantum computing technology continues to advance, we can expect to see significant improvements in AI capabilities and applications across various domains.
item.author: _fameux_

item.title: 您在科技行业的第一份工作是什么？ | What Was Your First Job in the Tech Industry?
item.description:
一旦暂停，codenewbieteam 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

一旦解除暂停，codenewbieteam 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，codenewbieteam 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 codenewbieteam 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 codenewbieteam：

降低 codenewbieteam 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 codenewbieteam 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Once suspended, codenewbieteam will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, codenewbieteam will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by codenewbieteam will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If codenewbieteam is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag codenewbieteam:

Make all posts by codenewbieteam less visible

codenewbieteam consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging codenewbieteam will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: ben

item.title: 变异观察者 | MutationObserver
item.description:
它提供了一种观察和响应 DOM 结构内发生的变化的方法。

当发生更改时，例如添加或删除节点，将使用描述已发生更改的 MutationRecord 对象列表调用回调函数。

它可用于实现广泛的功能，例如延迟加载内容、自动更新 UI 元素和实时数据同步。

让我通过一个例子来解释变异观察者。

我们的目标是在类别的部分高度发生变化时添加动画。 我们需要听那个操作

前后

为此，我们需要类别部分的参考。

 import React, { useRef } from 'react'; import { CategoryItems } from './category-items/category-items'; import { CategoryTitles } from './category-titles/category-titles'; import './categories.scss'; const Categories = () => { const categoriesRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null); return ( <div className="categories" ref={categoriesRef}> <CategoryTitles /> <CategoryItems /> </div> ); }; export { Categories };

准备好categoriesRef之后，我们可以创建mutationObserver结构。

首先，我们需要创建 mutationObserver

 const mutationObserver = new MutationObserver(async () => { console.log('mutationObserver operation'); });

这是我们的 mutationObserver 实例，我们将从useEffect中调用该实例

 useEffect(() => { if (categoriesRef.current) { mutationObserver.observe(categoriesRef.current, { childList: true, subtree: true, }); return () => { mutationObserver.disconnect(); }; } }, []);

  • 我们用categoriesRef.current定义了mutationObserver.observe ，它监听childList和subtree等参数，你也可以查看这个以获得更多参数。

  • 并且我们还在componentWillUnmount钩子上定义了mutationObserver.disconnect()用于组件关闭时的删除监听操作。

因此，当categoriesRef的高度发生变化时，我们将看到控制台日志。

 console.log('mutationObserver operation');

然后我们将添加动画处理程序代码块并定义一个 CSS 过渡到categoriesRef

 const mutationObserver = new MutationObserver(async () => { const categoryTitles = document.getElementById('categoryTitles') as HTMLDivElement | null; const categoryItems = document.getElementById('categoryItems') as HTMLDivElement | null; if (categoriesRef.current && categoryTitles && categoryItems) { if (categoryTitles.clientHeight > categoryItems.clientHeight) { categoriesRef.current.style.height = `${categoryTitles.clientHeight}px`; } else { categoriesRef.current.style.height = `${categoryItems.clientHeight}px`; } } });
 .categories { transition: height 200ms ease; }

瞧 🎉

来源



原文:

MutationObserver is a JavaScript API that allows developers to listen for changes to specific elements or nodes in the DOM (Document Object Model). It provides a way to observe and respond to changes that occur within the DOM structure.

The MutationObserver works by registering a callback function to observe a specific target element or node in the DOM. When a change occurs, such as a node being added or removed, the callback function is called with a list of MutationRecord objects that describe the changes that have occurred.

The MutationObserver API provides a powerful tool for building responsive web applications that need to react to changes in the DOM. It can be used to implement a wide range of features, such as lazy-loading content, auto-updating UI elements, and real-time data synchronization.

Let me explain mutation observer through an example.

Our aim is to add an animation when the category's section height changes. We need to listen that operation

before after

In order to do this we need a reference of the category section.

import React, { useRef } from 'react';
import { CategoryItems } from './category-items/category-items';
import { CategoryTitles } from './category-titles/category-titles';
import './categories.scss'; const Categories = () => { const categoriesRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null); return ( <div className="categories" ref={categoriesRef}> <CategoryTitles /> <CategoryItems /> </div> );
}; export { Categories };

After preparing the categoriesRef, we can create the mutationObserver structure.

First of all, we need to create mutationObserver

 const mutationObserver = new MutationObserver(async () => { console.log('mutationObserver operation'); });

This is our mutationObserver instance we will call this instance from useEffect

 useEffect(() => { if (categoriesRef.current) { mutationObserver.observe(categoriesRef.current, { childList: true, subtree: true, }); return () => { mutationObserver.disconnect(); }; } }, []);

  • We defined mutationObserver.observe with categoriesRef.current which listens to the parameters such as childList and subtree, also you can check this out for more parameters.

  • And also we define the mutationObserver.disconnect() on componentWillUnmount hook for delete listen operation when component closed.

Henceforward when the height change at categoriesRef we will see the console log.

 console.log('mutationObserver operation');

Then we will add the animation handler code piece and define a CSS transition to categoriesRef

 const mutationObserver = new MutationObserver(async () => { const categoryTitles = document.getElementById('categoryTitles') as HTMLDivElement | null; const categoryItems = document.getElementById('categoryItems') as HTMLDivElement | null; if (categoriesRef.current && categoryTitles && categoryItems) { if (categoryTitles.clientHeight > categoryItems.clientHeight) { categoriesRef.current.style.height = `${categoryTitles.clientHeight}px`; } else { categoriesRef.current.style.height = `${categoryItems.clientHeight}px`; } } });

.categories { transition: height 200ms ease;
}

voilà 🎉

Source
item.author: hasantezcan

item.title: 使用 Docker 层缓存改进构建时间 | Improving build times with Docker Layer Caching
item.description:
容器镜像构建中的一个重要概念是层缓存。 为了优化您的构建时间和简化您的 CI/CD 工作流程，理解这一点很重要。

在这篇文章中，我们将看看图层缓存的三个不同方面：

  1. 优化 dockerfiles 以重用最大层数。
  2. 使用--cache-from在两个不同图像之间进行层缓存。
  3. 使用 BuildKit 的内联缓存。

了解层

让我们看一个简单的 dockerfile 的例子：

 # pull base image FROM ubuntu:latest # install wget RUN apt-get update && \ apt-get -y install wget

在这里，我们可以观察到两层。 第一个拉取基础镜像，第二个安装 wget。

使用层缓存，我们的目标是在每次构建中构建最少数量的层，同时尽可能多地重复使用以前的层。 从本质上讲，我们正在努力减少构建过程中的步骤数。

优化 Dockerfile 以重用层

如果我们构建上面作为示例的简单 dockerfile，将需要几秒钟的时间来拉取基础镜像。 如果我们将第二层更改为：

 # install wget and nginx RUN apt-get update && \ apt-get -y install wget && \ apt-get -y nginx

然后重新构建，我们会看到再次运行的只是第二层，并不会再次拉取基础镜像。 这是因为第一层已被缓存并正在被重新使用。

但是，如果我们更改第一层，我们将使其之后的所有后续层无效。

 # pull different image FROM ubuntu:18.04 # install wget nginx RUN apt-get update && \ apt-get -y install wget && \ apt-get -y nginx

这一次，将重新构建第一层之后的所有层。

牢记这一点，我们应该始终尝试遵循以下两个最佳实践：

  1. 将不太可能更改的命令保留在 dockerfile 的开头。
  2. 经常更改的命令理想情况下应该靠近 dockerfile 的末尾。

在图像之间使用图层缓存

如果已经构建了一个镜像并且它与您自己的 dockerfile 共享一些层，您可以在--cache-from标志的帮助下将该镜像用作构建缓存的一部分。

例如：

 IMG="my-image" # Pull an existing image docker pull ${IMG}:old-ver docker build --cache-from ${IMG}:old-ver -t ${IMG}:new-ver .

上述方法的问题在于它需要我们先从远程注册表中拉取镜像。 通过使用 BuildKit 的内联缓存，我们可以将图像缓存到我们的本地注册表，以避免昂贵的拉取操作。

 export DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 # Build and cache image $ docker build --build-arg BUILDKIT_INLINE_CACHE=1 .



原文:

An important concept in container image building is layer-caching. It is important to understand in order to optimize your build times and to streamline your CI/CD workflows.

In this post, we'll be taking a look at three different aspects of layer-caching:

  1. Optimizing dockerfiles to re-use the maximum number of layers.
  2. Layer-caching between two different images using --cache-from.
  3. Using BuildKit's inline cache.

Understanding Layers

Let's take a look at an example of a simple dockerfile:

# pull base image
FROM ubuntu:latest

# install wget
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get -y install wget

Here, we can observe two layers. The first one pulls the base image, and the second one installs wget.

With layer caching, our aim is to build a minimal amount of layers in each build, while re-using previous layers as much as possible. In essence, we're trying to reduce the number of steps in our build process.

Optimizing Dockerfiles to re-use layers

If we build the simple dockerfile we took as an example above, it will take a few seconds for the base image to be pulled. If we change the second layer to:

# install wget and nginx
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get -y install wget && \
    apt-get -y nginx

And then re-build, we will see that only the second layer will be run again, and the base image will not be pulled again. This is because the first layer has been cached and is being re-used.

However, if we change our first layer, we will be invalidating all subsequent layers that come after it.

# pull different image
FROM ubuntu:18.04

# install wget nginx
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get -y install wget && \
    apt-get -y nginx

This time, all layers after the first layer will be built again.

Keeping this in mind, we should always try to follow the two following best practices:

  1. Keep commands that are not likely to change at the start of the dockerfile.
  2. Commands that are going to change often should ideally be near the end of the dockerfile.

Using layer-caching between Images

If there is an image that has already been built and it shares some of the layers with your own dockerfile, you can use that image as part of the build cache with the help of the --cache-from flag.

For example:

IMG="my-image"

# Pull an existing image
docker pull ${IMG}:old-ver

docker build --cache-from ${IMG}:old-ver -t ${IMG}:new-ver .

BuildKit Inline Cache

The problem with the above approach is that it requires us pulling an image from a remote registry first. By using BuildKit's inline cache, we can cache images to our local registry in order to avoid expensive pull operations.

export DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1

# Build and cache image
$ docker build --build-arg BUILDKIT_INLINE_CACHE=1 .

item.title: 05 berufliches Vorwärtskommen：Was ist dein Warum | 05 berufliches Vorwärtskommen: Was ist dein Warum
item.description:
穆斯塔法_A

发表于 3 月 23 日

莫恩莫恩，

慕达发

一旦暂停，amustafa16421 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

取消暂停后，amustafa16421 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，amustafa16421 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 amustafa16421 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 amustafa16421 的方法：

降低 amustafa16421 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 amustafa16421 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Mustafa_A

Posted on Mar 23

Moin Moin,

Im letzten Teil dieser Serie↗ sagte ich, dass wenn man sein „Warum“ kennt, es einfach ist einen Karriere-Weg ( Weg zum beruflichen Vorwärtskommen ) zu gehen. Gehörst du zu denjenigen, die sagen, dass sie ihr „Warum“ nicht kennen ?

Gehen wir in uns und schauen woher diese Ungewissheit kommt. Vielleicht denkst du dir, dass Geld dich nicht antreiben darf, oder Ansehen, oder eine Veränderung in der Welt zu bewirken.

  • Würdest du das Geld für gute Zwecke nutzen ?
  • Stell dir vor, dass du dein Ansehen nutzt, um Arbeitsbedingungen für Software-Entwickler zu verbessern
  • Wärst du erfüllt, wenn du dich nie drum bemühen würdest, die Veränderung zu erreichen ?

Vielleicht gab es bei dir einen bestimmten Moment in deiner Kindheit oder in deiner Jugend, der dazu führte, dass du diesen Weg gegangen bist. Besinne dich darauf und schöpfe daraus Kraft.

Beste Grüße,
Mustafa

Once suspended, amustafa16421 will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, amustafa16421 will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by amustafa16421 will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If amustafa16421 is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag amustafa16421:

Make all posts by amustafa16421 less visible

amustafa16421 consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct
